reviews.review_description:
The game kind of reminds me of bioshock and even just that feels awesome
I really enjoyed the game.

There was a couple of crashes that I did experience. Especially at the end maybe just because there's so much going on.

I really enjoyed how the weapons look different.
The more you upgraded them and how they did feel stronger i do feel feel like the left hand was kind of missed out on combat or utility Besides the three abilities that you get but that's just me being picky.

The environments were good sometimes a bit confusing
But it definitely did feel like a completely different area Each time which also includes the bosses.I liked their design a lot.

The only thing that I guess I can say I disliked was i finished it 

I do hope this game gets more attention. It is a very fun game and I kind of want another one great jobbb

I had never heard of this game, and after seeing the good reviews, I decided to roll the dice. I’ve made this wager a dozen times before (probably more) and it almost always ends in disappointment. But not this time. This game defied all expectations. Beating the game was an immersive adventure. Whoever made this put a lot of love and care into it.

reviews.review_title: this game is one of the best
reviews.review_description: It seems crazy to me that this game isn't near the top of everybody's "best of" lists. It's fantastic. I did encounter that one bug that a few other people have mentioned, where the room didn't fill with water after pulling the lever. Didn't require reinstalling the game, didn't require rebooting the headset, just required shutting down the game and restarting it. That bug only happened a couple of times, and every time it was fixed just by restarting the game real quick, not a big deal. Good variety of enemies, good level design, good variety of weapons, weapons feel good to use, good voice acting, competently written story, excellent graphics for standalone. I actually had to turn the difficulty down from "normal" to "easy" about halfway through the game, it's satisfyingly challenging and I look forward to replaying it in a month or three at a higher difficulty level. If you like single-player story-driven games, this is one of the best available on the Quest. There's only a handful of games that come close to the level of RE4, and this is one of them.

reviews.review_title: Best game I have played in a long time.
reviews.review_description: Underrated game that I only heard about by somebody mentioning it on YouTube. During the Meta holiday sale I decided to pull the trigger because the price was right. What a ride this is! The game works perfectly with one known glitch towards the end (water main handle) which is an easy fix with a reinstall. Aside from that I highly recommend as it has great replayability. One of the most perfect games out there. Devs please fix the glitch, I love this game!

Guys, really, this game is amazing!! I addicted since I bought last month I just cann't stop playing. Congratulations! I didn't finish yet, but so far I'm in love. 
I had wrote about been stuck in pump rooms at maintenance area due a bug where when you pulled the lever the room doenst flood with water. But never mind. I managed pass after reinstalled the game!! Anyway Congratulations again, wonderfull job with this game!
Great game play and wonderful graphics. Puzzles to solve and plenty of nasty creatures to dispose of but none so difficult to make it annoying. Great job by the devs look forward to more.
One of the most underrared games ever. Very very good!
If you enjoy story driven games that are creatively designed with smooth gameplay, this is for you.
I love this kind of atmosphere so much. Gameplay, difficulty, saving, everything done right. Please make a second part!

reviews.review_title: Feels like a nostalgic VR experience
reviews.review_description: This game is simply fun and feels like a blast from the past. For some reason it reminds me of VR Alien version of Bioshock. I often find it hard to get into games and this is one I look forward to getting back into when the kids go to bed.

Genotype is exactly the kind of games I was looking for! I'm gonna give it 5 stars because despite its small flaws I REALLY enjoyed and loved every second of it.

Gameplay, exploration, story, music, shooting, keycards, safe-codes, it's got everything!

My only issues with the game were a huge game-breaking bug in the Maintenance level that had the lever for the water rooms not work (but rebooting my Quest 3 fixed the issue) and also switching weapons (even tho I love playing with the wire when I'm not in a hurry, it can be a bit too hard mid-combat, and rockets are even worse).

Definitely recommend this game to fans of games like Prey (2017)
Great metroidvania. Enemies a bit of damage Sponges on normal but perfect on easy imo

This game surprised me. The presentation and graphics are really good and not what we usually find on indie games like this (to me it looks much better on the headset than on videos). I've initially played on PCVR, and when I've tried on Quest 3 I couldn't notice much difference. Interactions feel good, the different types of "guns" are cool and immersive, and the metroidvania formula of exploring the rooms in the facility and progression feels great. My only gripe is that enemies seem to respawn every time you return to an area, but since there is not much ammo around you are kind of out-of-luck if you decide to go to a different section than the one that the game intended you to go (so you need to make sure to always look at the map to verify where you are going, since going to the wrong area may mean you won't have enough ammo for the enemies).

Masterpiece 
Originally i didn’t expect much but blown away on how much fun and immersive this game is. 

Pros: game length seems pretty Hefty. Metrovania style with teleport rooms.

Controls are extremely easy to manage and feel fluid and naturally smooth.

Tons of weaponized organism guns with each their own 2nd alternative version to use during upgrading and resetting anytime to use the upgrades you need. Like turning one gun from semi auto to full auto or shotgun. 

The story is creepy and fun.

The music provides atmosphere that is perfectly scored for the theme of this game.

Graphics are better than alien rogue vr meta version. If this team ever makes future graphics updates i will be even more blown away.

The crafting system I love. And lost items. 

Boss battles feel great with the options of using micro missiles. 

Already up to the 4th map and tons left to unlock so ngth wise brilliantly created game.

I appreciate the work and art it took to make this game possible, you guys might be my new fav VR devs. I look forward to whats next from this game and your future projects  dlc.


Cons: im picky but cant find a con.

Wish list:
Like many metrovanias itd be nice to have a boss rush mode and hidden surprises and rewards for completing the game. 

Fighting the aliens in the snow in day time and nightime outside to see how great the lighting would look

Future games, with your talents, definitely more metrovanias which were always a big fav of mine. Id love to see one day a ratchet and clank style clone game  with such great mechanics. And more open world games. Impressed this runs smooth with big maps on quest 3.thanks ill keep an eye out for your future.

First off, it was so nice to play an action/adventure game 
without having to shoot people. That gets old. In this 
game you’re interacting with weird creatures, still deadly
but at least not misanthropic. 

The graphics are interesting, believable and smooth. The mechanics are smart and seamless and natural. The
storyline is engaging and makes sense.  The devs
made this thing right so that it’s actually fun to play!
Genotype is up there with any triple A game.
The gameplay is challenging, but the storyline and action make it well worth it even if you have to start back at checkpoints a few times.

Way to frustrating when you play for 20 minutes and die loose progress. Save points far and few between. Changing weapons are a joke and take way too long. Ammo and health scarce. Just poor layout. 
Won’t be played again.

Whilst I enjoy the game there just doesn't seem to be enough ammo. Enemies respawn and I'm spending all currency on ammo but I still keep running out mid fight. It kills the enjoyment when I have to let the enemies kill me because I can't fight back. The level design is also fairly basic. Good voice acting though.

C'est un très très bon jeu avec une bonne durée de vie. Les graphismes sont excellents sur le Q3,  l'ambiance est là et l'histoire est bien. J'ai eu des petits bugs au niveau de la submersion de l'eau. Des fois la poignée ne fonctionnait pas et puis c'est revenu. Je vous conseille ce jeu qui est dans la même lignée que Red Matter avec une touche d'action de plus. N'hésitez pas, achetez le....

EDIT: honestly still one of the better games on the Quest that never really got the attention it deserved. with all the new Quest owners because of games like Batman Meta really should push this game towards the front of the store instead keeping it towards the back. It is one of your higher rated games in the APP store Meta. 

I was so interested in these developers because I wanted to keep an eye out for new projects that I looked into them to find out they are a Danish start up. Strange because my mother is from Copenhagen not that anyone cares.


                               Review 
￼
I don’t normally write reviews but this game definitely deserves its recognition. There are only 3 maybe 4 games a year that are released on the quest that really sets its hooks into me. This is definitely one of them. I like VR the problem for me is finding good games. Unfortunately there’s not a lot of quality content imo.  So I really look forward to games like this that pull me back into VR. The game itself looks really good. It also played smooth with only a couple minor hiccups. The game  takes place in the south pole/ Antarctica, in a secret, abandoned base that feels more like an alien planet. The game definitely has some Metroid prime vibes, but stands all on its own. The polished look and gameplay along with the graphic style of the environment, with the music really sets the ambience. A lot of the enemies reminded me something straight out of Metroid prime, along with the glowing foliage and sci-fi soundtrack. Along the way, you will be able to upgrade your weapons and gloves which will unlock new pathways and different doors. This is a definite five star game in a must-have if you like story driven campaigns RPG’s,  FPS, or sci-fi. Depending on how you play the game you can expect a good 6 to 8 hours of gameplay. The devs managed to do this while only taking up 1.5 GB of space. These devs definitely know something. This game deserves to be on the top 10 selling list.  To the developers, Thanks hank you for getting me excited about  VR again.

OK this might just be my favorite quest stand alone game. I'm playing on a quest 2 and the graphics are on level with red matter 2 (pinnacle of quest graphics imo). It has a true campaign with an engaging story and atmosphere. 

I'm not really getting the "metrovania" thing they advertise, but to me that's a plus. I don't want to get lost and backtrack forever. This game has a great map and I feel like I know what I'm supposed to be doing. 

I also appreciate the Barney Calhoun security reference so.....maybe the half life vibes were intentional.

This is a must have for quest owners looking for an engaging campaign as opposed to the horde mode/arena shooters plaguing the quest store. 

Amazing

** edit
I just finished the game, its a masterpiece. Best game on quest and tied with best vr game (half-life alyx). 

I will revist this game probably every year, its that good. Thank you, for making such an amazing experience.

At first it took some time to get used to to the bugs constant attacks, but over time I gotthe hang of it. The use of different created “bug” weapons was unique. I also enjoyed the semi free range maps. I finished the game but still have a couple of things I’m goin track to complete. I thought I was close to completing the game about half way through and was surprised that there was so much more to get through!
Great game!!

If you just ran out of ammo, then you basically need to drop every progress and start from the very beginning.
I am stuck in the beginning of the game, I'm not skill enough to kill the bugs with my rest ammo, then you don't get any progress on this game.
I have to start from beginning due to I cannot pass the simple level, and the only way seems to be restart again...
It's frustrating.

reviews.review_title: I used to love it  before the update
reviews.review_description: So this game was pretty awesome and the thing I truly liked about it was that it wasn't as much of a combat game as it was a thriller and it was perfect just the way it was. I worked all day and was excited to play some when I got home and put my headset on to find that they updated the game and ruined it overnight. I have spent many hours playing this game and when I tried to resume my saved game I was informed that they pretty much charged the game and I would be better off starting the whole game over than trying to resume, and I quickly found out why. Most creators make some cool after thought updates that Don't mess up your saved game progress and totally change the game. I was originally really impressed on how I wasn't constantly having to shoot things which truly added to the level of surprise when you actually did have to. It's what I loved about the game. So unless you're going to make it possible to get my old game back the way it should have stayed, I will never play this game again or anything else produced by these developers

The gameplay after this update is spectacular! The graphics are crisp and the voice acting is something out of a movie! The guy William voice is perfect for the story he is nerdy scary. The environment is very cool with the game and everything you need is explained! Weapons are eco/-criter dope! I enjoy this baby thanks  to the whole team behind the game! Cheers

I just finished the game right now and I loved it. The only bad thing for me is that I am Spanish and although I understood most of the game, the lack of subtitles in Spanish or at least in English made some parts of the game and some parts of the lore difficult for me. Anyway, I really enjoyed the gameplay, graphics, atmosphere and everything! Well done!

developer_response.body: Hi Alex, thank you for the kind words! You  may have to return to Antarctica, because we are releasing a patch, that has subtitles in a number of different languages, including Spanish. We hope to have the patch out tonight, if all goes well :-)

reviews.review_description:
First off let's talk about the positives, even though their aren't many.

(Pro's)
Looks great on quest 3.
Decent voice acting.
A few cool creature designs 

Alright so with those 3 pieces out of the way, let's get into the problems with this game. 
First and foremost they just released the "combat" update and I'm sorry but this is literally the worst combat I have ever experienced in VR by far, the hit detection is absolutely horrible, and the combat is just plain boring and outright un-fun.  
You have an alien straped to your wrist shooting living projectiles which should be awesome but it's just straight up boring.  
The levels are the most basic cookie cutter syfy fair ever, they have no substance, nothing particularly interesting and the layout is once again just boring, this is basically a corridor shooter with boring combat and boring level designs and all of that equates to a just straight up boring game. 
At the end of the day I would honestly prefer a broken/bad game that is actively trying to be interesting then an experience that is just utterly boring and forgettable.

1/5

reviews.review_title: A fun Metroidvania that you can’t ignore
reviews.review_description:
I just finished AW2 and I was looking for a new full length game (that isn’t a roguelike) and Genotype came from the left field amaze me.

I can’t help but compare the two games together, being on the adrenaline rush that AW2 offered, and between the two Genotype is just missing the “sense of grandeur” and the extensive lore of AW2 but everything else is on par or better in Genotype.

Genotype is a true modern Metroidvania (more of the Metroid part than the Vania part). The exploration is what’s shine in the game, and the fights don’t get in the way of it, even if this means that the pacing is a little weird sometimes. The bosses aren’t that hard, I wish they moved a bit more or varied their attacks, but they still fit the pacing and theme of the game. If I could I would remove half a star to the rating, over the bosses… most times you wonder if they were bosses or just larger versions of the other critters.

The UI is simple by to the point, it has to me the most fluid interactions in any VR games I have played. You want a map on your left while exploring? Just look left and press a button! Not that there isn’t annoyances. For example, the inventory only shows the bottom of each items facing the played, making it hard to know what everything is sometimes. Still, the interactions with objects in the game is just well designed. The whole world can be explored without ever opening a menu screen…

And then there is the weapon system, which is just as inspired as the UI. In the beginning it’s weird and it looks childish, but then you realize how ideal everything is for VR. You want to grab something with your gun holding hand? Just do it! The gun is not in the way! You don’t have to hold the gun, being forced to hold the grab button all the like in most other games, which just shows how much they thought about it. I still have cramps from holding the axe in AW2…

What’s more, this game makes scavenging fun again. The way you use items is quick and painless, the loot container have doors and latches made for VR interaction (you never have to fight with a handle trying to pull open any doors, a plague in most other games). There is a million details that show how much they thought about everything.

And finally the graphics… I play on the Quest 2 and I was just aghast at the interiors. It not only feels like a sci-fi base, it looks like it in ways that is just amazing. Everything is so blocky, with sharp edges, obviously for the poly count, but it’s integrated in the art style so well. The base is some brutalist inspired architecture, every props are brutalist, etc. They married theme and performance seamlessly to work together.

Tl;dr: This game is fun and worth any gamers time, especially if you like Metroidvanias. It’s not a hardcore shooter, but it’s not a boring one either. Expect to walk around a lot.
lots to explore, i like the different missions. the space lab is cool. worth the money, good graphics.

I loved the puzzle and action aspect of the game. Decent weapon selection and boss fights would recommend  higher difficulty as late game weapons with upgrades make bosses a bit easy.

 Also, I only ran into 3 notable bugs: falling through the floor twice, messages for clues not spawning,  and after opening the workshop couldn't go in like a forcefully prevented me from entering

developer_response.body: Hi Bhatchet :D Have you had a chance to try out Genotype since we released our combat update? A "new game" should result in a WAY more satisfying experience ... And maybe even a 5-star rating. ;D

reviews.review_title: Pass if u can I tried to get a refund
reviews.review_description: Game needs some serious redesigning, not nearly anuff money to stay alive health is expensive and only 35 percent increase, waves of bugs behind every door , 1 hit ur dead unless u have 100 percent health which is rare as the health cost way too much as others have said . Game is not designed well as developers messed up the trade and buying program. It's not fun to die so much because of poor development of the game. It's some what entertaining till u see u can't progress in certain rooms and areas cuzz of these poor design problems of fair play . Update I feel like I was a bit too harsh with the design team adding a new patch with nice adjustments, they are responding and I appreciate that and their efforts I will give it another go hopefully I can advance this time , but gosh normal play was extemely difficult and can hardly believe they added 2 harder levels yikes so I'll go on easy and see if I got a chance haha probably not. Update after new modes, well as suspected there is no easy mode as stated, still waves of creatures in every room , u get over run fast of course they never miss drain ur health in one shot they shoot fast ur out numbered same old game nothing easier about easy honesty I couldn't tell the update even happened, did not find any ammo or health lying around. I would enjoy a more casual play thru and just deal with puzzles and not  2 4 or 5 creatures in one small room and doors lock behind u every time so no backing out regrouping to form a strategy,  seriously the doors locking on u forcing u to do battle without retreat and get a advantage is really lame come on seriously, who thought if that death trap again not a fair playing field. As many have said game is still way too difficult and u added 2 more even more difficult modes haha seriously.  So beware all easy is hard and normal is impossible. Game still needs more.adjustments . I've play so many games twd re4 into the radius etc and all those have been a level playing field. Sorry but needs a lot more adjustments .
I think this is a great game. Initially I was annoyed that the devs did not include difficulty settings. They have since remedied that . 5  out of 5 stars . Tremendous game
Died had to start over. I'm very annoyed. I may return this game

developer_response.body:
EDIT: Hi Vr lover, did you manage to figure out how to save using the Check-In Consoles? Kind regards Rasmus.
 ---- 
Hi Vr lover. Rasmus here (Arjay on the official Bolverk Games Discord). Sounds like you had a rough start at the South Pole. Spread out across the base are save-terminals (so called Check-In Consoles). The first one is located near where you found the Grubber. They are green-ish terminals, on which you save your progress. You can also adjust the difficulty setting if you find combat challenging. Have a nice day. :)

reviews.review_title: What an amazing surprise thanks Bolverk!
reviews.review_description:
Genotype is freaking minted.The game’s graphics are stunning, with the environment being rendered in great detail. The sound design is also impressive, with the howling winds and creaking metal of the base adding to the immersion of the game

Overall, Genotype is an excellent game that will keep you on the edge of your feet. The graphics and sound design are top-notch, and the gameplay is challenging but rewarding. If you’re looking for a survival game with an Antarctic theme, then Genotype is definitely worth checking out.

Warning: Don’t start this game unless you have some time to play cause it is seriously addictive! It’s the most fun I’ve had in VR. I get so engrossed in the story and the atmospheric graphics that it surprises me when I remove my headset and I’m in my house! I would rate it 10 stars if I could. GREAT job Bolverk!

PS: The penguin is adorable.

developer_response.body: Hi mitZEE! Rasmus here (Arjay on Discord). I am afraid that despite your warnings, new explorers are still streaming to the South Pole every day. However, we appreciate your diligence, so please enjoy this free penguin.🐧 Jokes aside, thank you so much for your kind words. It makes it worth all the hard work. All the best from Rasmus and the whole Bolverk Games team. :)

reviews.review_title: It is a good game, but kind repetititive
reviews.review_description: A good game, but it’s kind of repetitive whenever you start getting halfway done. I haven’t finished it so I can’t give you a full good review but it kind of got repetitive but it’s a good game.

I highly recommend Genotype. I think it's one of the best games on Quest right now. Came way out of left field with the premise but I promise you it holds up. Very atmospheric, very well told story. Half-life-1-esque but with a modern feel.

Love this game. Although I can't seem to get far before I die. But it's one of those games that keeps you coming back for more. Also there is fighting strange looking bugs and swimming and crafting items and a cute penguin what more could you ask for.

This game is fun and the story is interesting just diving in now but having fun.  There are a few gripes and I have a few tips to make this a 5 star game
1. Assign creature slots to rip cord as trying to find fav weapons in a fight is tricky.
2. Gruber aim sucks. Better to use grip for a slashing whip and trigger for grab for a more fun and useful tool/melee weapon.
3. Dropped items shouldn't disappeared. You can retrieve them at terminal but why.
4. Interactive objects for a more realistic environment. Pick up cups, grab items ect. It's vr why not.
5. Never liked the either/or upgrade trees, if you got the funds why not buy them all make it more fun.
6. Left hand rocket should have dedicated slot so you can still access meds and tokens. Trying to equip one in a fight gets you killed.
7. Ammo consumption is brutal. Let them drop ammo or more ammo to find.
8. Swimming feels unatural. Just put in a simpler swimming gesture or joystick but weird grabbing motions that don't move intuitively ain't working. Maybe a critter that aids in aquapropultion.
9. Patch the glitches. Unlocked doors not opening until room is left. Enemies clipping outside walls. Boss music starting when returning to room.

developer_response.body:
Reply:

Hi Candrews. Rasmus from Bolverk here (Arjay on Discord).
We have have released a patch, where we try to fix a few of the issues that you raise:

We’ve introduced four difficulty settings that are changeable at any time during a playthrough.
More loot is now scattered around the facility (extra ammo, health, and other useful items). 
We have also made a number of alterations to the genomass prices, item durations and more.
Several environments in the game have been updated, giving them a richer feel.
Also, we have made improvements to melee behavior of the jumping enemies, and their ability to hit the player, to make them more realistic.

Hopefully the changes will make you consider a second trip to the South Pole? :) Thank you so much for helping us make Genotype even better!

reviews.review_description: Great!
